fields.label: Company Name
fields.consultant_note: Official ACRA-registered name — I'll cross-reference eligibility against government databases.

fields.label: Industry / Sector
fields.consultant_note: Different grants target different sectors. Food businesses have dedicated funds. Tech has IMDA/EDB support. Tell me exactly what you do.

fields.label: Local Shareholding %
fields.consultant_note: This is critical. Most Enterprise Singapore grants require ≥30% local shareholding. Below that, your options narrow significantly.

fields.label: Number of Employees (Singapore)
fields.consultant_note: SME definition: ≤200 employees OR ≤S$100M annual turnover. Some workforce grants scale with headcount.

fields.label: Annual Revenue (S$)
fields.consultant_note: Revenue determines SME vs non-SME status. Non-SMEs lose access to PSG, MRA, and many other grants.

fields.label: Years in Business
fields.consultant_note: Startup grants (<3 years) vs growth grants (3-10 years) vs expansion grants (10+). Different doors open at different stages.

fields.label: What do you need help with? (Select all that apply)
fields.consultant_note: The more specific you are, the more targeted my recommendations. A food manufacturer wanting to automate is a very different grant path from one wanting to export.

fields.label: Have you received government grants before?
fields.consultant_note: Some grants have stacking rules — you can't double-claim for the same expense. I'll flag conflicts.

fields.label: Anything specific you're looking for?
fields.consultant_note: Free text. Tell me what's actually on your mind — specific grant names, pain points, timelines.

fields.label: Company Name
fields.consultant_note: Your Business Registration Certificate name — required for all HK government schemes.

fields.label: Industry / Sector
fields.consultant_note: HK has dedicated funds for trading, manufacturing, and tech. Exporters get extra support.

fields.label: Business Registration Status
fields.consultant_note: Must have a valid Business Registration Certificate for most schemes.

fields.label: Number of Employees (Hong Kong)
fields.consultant_note: Under HK's SME definition: <100 employees (manufacturing) or <50 (non-manufacturing).

fields.label: Years in Business
fields.consultant_note: BUD Fund and EMF are accessible early. TIP requires operational history.

fields.label: What do you need help with?
fields.consultant_note: HK grants are heavily export/GBA-focused. BUD Fund and EMF are your best friends for market expansion.

fields.label: Do you make MPF contributions for HK employees?
fields.consultant_note: Some workforce schemes require MPF contributions as proof of genuine HK operations.

fields.label: Anything specific you're looking for?
fields.consultant_note: BUD Fund? SME Loan Guarantee? Tell me what you've heard about or are curious about.

fields.label: Company Name
fields.consultant_note: Your business registration name. Required for all government schemes.

fields.label: Industry / Sector
fields.consultant_note: Indonesia has dedicated programs for manufacturing, agriculture, and halal certification.

fields.label: Enterprise Type
fields.consultant_note: UMKM vs PMA — different rulebooks. Some grants are domestic-only.

fields.label: UMKM Size Classification
fields.consultant_note: Micro enterprises get priority for digitalisation and KUR loans.

fields.label: Number of Employees
fields.consultant_note: Indonesia micro ≤4, small 5-19, medium 20-99.

fields.label: Years in Business
fields.consultant_note: KUR loans accessible from day 1. LINK & Match requires at least 1 year.

fields.label: What do you need help with?
fields.consultant_note: Bantuan Digitalisasi UMKM covers digital. BRI KUR for microfinance. LINK & Match for industry partnerships. Kartu Prakerja for training.

fields.label: Anything specific you're looking for?
fields.consultant_note: KUR loan? Halal certification? BPOM registration? Kartu Prakerja? Specific is better.

fields.label: Company Name
fields.consultant_note: SSM-registered name. I'll check your SME status against SME Corp definitions.

fields.label: Industry / Sector
fields.consultant_note: Halal certification support, automation grants for manufacturing, MSC status for tech — Malaysia has industry-specific schemes.

fields.label: Bumiputera Status
fields.consultant_note: This matters enormously. TERAJU, MARA, PUNB, and many state grants are Bumiputera-specific. Non-Bumi and foreign companies have different grant pathways.

fields.label: SME Classification (SME Corp)
fields.consultant_note: SME Corp redefined thresholds in 2026. Manufacturing: revenue ≤RM50M or ≤200 staff. Services: revenue ≤RM20M or ≤75 staff.

fields.label: Years in Business
fields.consultant_note: Startup grants (like SUPERB, CIP Spark) vs growth grants (like ITG, MDEC GAIN).

fields.label: What do you need help with?
fields.consultant_note: Malaysia has strong automation grants (MATRADE, MITI) and the world's only dedicated Halal ecosystem support.

fields.label: Export-Oriented?
fields.consultant_note: MATRADE has dedicated export development grants not available to domestic-only businesses.

fields.label: Anything specific you're looking for?
fields.consultant_note: TERAJU? MATRADE MDG? SME Bank financing? Tell me what you need.

fields.label: Company Name
fields.consultant_note: DBD-registered name. I'll check your BOI and SME status.

fields.label: Industry / Sector
fields.consultant_note: BOI targets specific industries for promotion. S-Curve and New S-Curve sectors get the best incentives.

fields.label: BOI Promotion Status
fields.consultant_note: BOI promotion unlocks tax holidays, work permit fast-tracks, and land ownership rights. If you're not BOI-promoted, that's a priority recommendation.

fields.label: Thai Shareholding %
fields.consultant_note: Foreign Business Act restricts some sectors. BOI promotion can override FBA restrictions. Critical for eligibility.

fields.label: Number of Employees (Thailand)
fields.consultant_note: Thai SME definition: manufacturing ≤200 staff or ≤฿500M revenue. Services ≤100 staff or ≤฿300M revenue.

fields.label: What do you need help with?
fields.consultant_note: BOI is the gateway to most major incentives. DEPA covers digital. OSMEP covers SME development.

fields.label: Anything specific you're looking for?
fields.consultant_note: BOI tax holiday? DEPA digital grant? SME Bank loan? Specific is better.

fields.label: Company Name
fields.consultant_note: Your Enterprise Registration Certificate name — required for all government schemes.

fields.label: Industry / Sector
fields.consultant_note: Priority sectors (high-tech, green energy, agriculture) get the best tax incentives. Location matters as much as sector.

fields.label: Enterprise Type
fields.consultant_note: This is the most important question for Vietnam. FDI companies face different rules than domestic enterprises. Some grants are domestic-only.

fields.label: Province / Location
options: Binh Duong / Dong Nai (Southern industrial zone)
Northern industrial provinces (Bac Ninh, Hai Phong, etc.)
fields.consultant_note: Provincial incentives vary enormously. Difficult/special economic zones offer CIT rates as low as 10%. HCMC and Hanoi have different schemes than rural provinces.

fields.label: Number of Employees
fields.consultant_note: Vietnam SME definition: micro ≤10, small ≤100, medium ≤200. Different grant tiers.

fields.label: What do you need help with?
fields.consultant_note: NATIF covers technology. SME Development Fund covers financing. Provincial People's Committees handle local incentives.

fields.label: Technology Level
fields.consultant_note: High-tech enterprises get the best CIT rates (10% for 15 years) and priority access to NATIF.

fields.label: Anything specific you're looking for?
fields.consultant_note: NATIF funding? CIT holiday? Provincial land incentives? Tell me.

fields.label: Company Name
fields.consultant_note: SEC or DTI registered name. Registration type determines your grant access.

fields.label: Industry / Sector
fields.consultant_note: DOST SETUP targets manufacturing/processing. DTI OTOP targets regional products. IT-BPO has PEZA programs.

fields.label: Business Registration
fields.consultant_note: Sole props have different eligibility than corporations. Cooperatives have dedicated programs.

fields.label: Barangay Micro Business Enterprise (BMBE)
fields.consultant_note: BMBE registration is the lowest-hanging fruit in the Philippines — instant income tax exemption and minimum wage exemption. If you qualify and haven't registered, that's your first step.

fields.label: Number of Employees
fields.consultant_note: Philippine SME definition: micro ≤9 staff & ≤₱3M assets, small ≤99 & ≤₱15M, medium ≤199 & ≤₱100M.

fields.label: What do you need help with?
fields.consultant_note: DOST SETUP is the flagship — grants for equipment. SB Corp handles microfinance (P3/RISE UP). PEZA/BOI handle tax incentives.

fields.label: PEZA / BOI Registration
fields.consultant_note: PEZA/BOI registration unlocks income tax holidays (4-7 years) and duty-free imports. Worth pursuing for exporters and strategic industries.

fields.label: Anything specific you're looking for?
fields.consultant_note: DOST SETUP? P3/RISE UP loan? BMBE registration? PEZA incentives? Tell me.

fields.label: Company Name
fields.consultant_note: Your ABN-registered business name. Required for all government schemes.

fields.label: Industry / Sector
fields.consultant_note: Australia has dedicated programs for mining, agritech, defence, space, and clean energy. Industry-specific grants are the moat.

fields.label: State / Territory
fields.consultant_note: Each state runs its own grant programs — completely separate from federal. NSW has MVP Ventures, VIC has LaunchVic, QLD has Ignite Ideas. Your state IS your moat.

fields.label: Annual Turnover (A$)
fields.consultant_note: Critical for R&D Tax Incentive: under A$20M turnover = 43.5% refundable offset. Over A$20M = 38.5% non-refundable. This is the single most valuable Australian grant.

fields.label: Number of Employees (Australia)
fields.consultant_note: SME definition: <200 employees OR <A$10M turnover. Different grants have different thresholds.

fields.label: Years in Business
fields.consultant_note: Early Stage Innovation Company (ESIC) tax incentives for young companies. R&D Tax Incentive has no minimum age.

fields.label: Indigenous Business Status
fields.consultant_note: Indigenous Business Australia provides dedicated loans and support. Supply Nation registration opens procurement doors. If Indigenous-owned, this is a significant advantage.

fields.label: Export-Oriented?
fields.consultant_note: Export Market Development Grant (EMDG) reimburses up to 50% of export promotion spend. Austrade and Export Finance Australia have dedicated export financing.

fields.label: What do you need help with? (Check all that apply)
options: R&D and innovation (tax offset or grant)
fields.consultant_note: Australia's R&D Tax Incentive is the crown jewel — 43.5% cash refund for small companies. EMDG for exporters. CSIRO Kick-Start for research. State grants vary enormously.

fields.label: Anything specific you're looking for?
fields.consultant_note: R&D Tax Incentive? EMDG? CSIRO Kick-Start? NSW MVP Grant? Specific is better.
